relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Levite family heads approach priest, Joshua, and tribal leaders.
They ask for cities and pasturelands promised through Moses.
Israel gives the landless servants actual places to live.
Aaron's priestly descendants receive thirteen cities by lot.
The remaining Kohathites receive ten cities.
Gershon receives thirteen cities across northern tribes and Bashan.
Merari receives twelve cities from Reuben, Gad, and Zebulun.
The distribution fulfills the command through Moses.
Judah and Simeon provide named cities.
The first lot belongs to Aaron's line.
Hebron is given with pasturelands, though its fields remain Caleb's.
Caleb's possession is preserved beside Levitical dwelling.
Hebron becomes both priestly city and refuge for the manslayer.
Jattir and Eshtemoa join the priestly list.
Holon and Debir are added with pasturelands.
Ain, Juttah, and Beth-shemesh complete nine from Judah and Simeon.
Benjamin gives Gibeon and Geba.
Anathoth and Almon complete the Benjamin group.
Aaron's descendants receive thirteen cities.
The remaining Kohathites receive their Ephraim allotment.
Shechem becomes another refuge city with pasturelands.
Kibzaim and Beth-horon complete four Ephraim cities.
Dan gives Elteke and Gibbethon.
Aijalon and Gath-rimmon complete Dan's four.
Half Manasseh gives Taanach and Gath-rimmon.
The remaining Kohathite total is ten cities.
Gershon receives Golan as refuge and Beeshterah in Bashan.
Issachar gives Kishion and Daberath.
Jarmuth and En-gannim complete Issachar's four.
Asher gives Mishal and Abdon.
Helkath and Rehob complete Asher's four.
Naphtali gives Kedesh as refuge, plus Hammoth-dor and Kartan.
Gershon's total is thirteen cities.
Merari receives Jokneam and Kartah from Zebulun.
Dimnah and Nahalal complete Zebulun's four.
Reuben gives Bezer and Jahaz.
Kedemoth and Mephaath complete Reuben's four.
Gad gives Ramoth as refuge and Mahanaim.
Heshbon and Jazer complete Gad's four.
Merari's total is twelve cities.
The Levite network totals forty-eight cities with pasturelands.
Every city is remembered with land around it for living creatures.
The narrator declares the land promise given and inhabited.
Rest is announced around Israel.
The good word is declared fulfilled.

contemplative_note: Joshua 21 insists that spiritual vocation still needs housing, pasture, and material support. Refuge cities inside the Levite network make service inseparable from protection. Yet the chapter's fulfillment language must remain in tension with the book's own memory of surviving, displaced, and subordinated peoples.
